Title: Logs tab
Summary: Server side log buffer. Captures console.{log,info,warn,error} since boot.
Body:
## What it captures

A 1000 entry ring buffer monkey patches `console.{log,info,warn,error}` at module load (it must be the very first import in the server entry, so boot lines are captured too). Every server side log line lands here.

## What this tab shows

Newest first, with a three way filter: All, Good (info only), Bad (warning + error). On first open, if there are any warning or error entries, the filter defaults to Bad so you land on relevant lines instead of scrolling.

Refresh and Clear buttons in the header. Clear wipes the buffer (useful for "give me a clean slate before reproducing a bug").

## What is here vs the Saves tab

Save flow errors flow through both: the save itself appears in the Saves activity feed (with a warning or error outcome), and the underlying error lands in Logs. Same root cause, two paths. The intentional double counting is because the user's path to fix is different in each case ("see the broken save in context" vs "see the raw error message").

## What is not here yet

No SSE streaming. New errors after page load do not update the header icon until you reload. Adding SSE plus a virtualized live feed is a substantial follow up; the current shape captures the core value (history plus filter) at a fraction of the cost.
